title: Grammar: Preterite vs. Imperfect
description: Understand when to use completed actions versus ongoing background actions.

sentences.spanish: Ayer llovía cuando salí.
sentences.english: Yesterday it was raining when I left.

sentences.spanish: Yo leía mientras él dormía.
sentences.english: I was reading while he was sleeping.

sentences.spanish: Hacía sol y fuimos a la playa.
sentences.english: It was sunny and we went to the beach.

sentences.spanish: Eran las tres cuando llegaron.
sentences.english: It was three o'clock when they arrived.

sentences.spanish: Estaba cansado, así que me fui.
sentences.english: I was tired, so I left.

sentences.spanish: Conocí a mi esposa en Madrid.
sentences.english: I met my wife in Madrid.

sentences.spanish: Yo sabía la verdad pero no dije nada.
sentences.english: I knew the truth but didn't say anything.

sentences.spanish: De niño, jugaba en el parque.
sentences.english: As a child, I used to play in the park.

sentences.spanish: Hablamos por dos horas anoche.
sentences.english: We talked for two hours last night.

sentences.spanish: Quería salir, pero empezó a llover.
sentences.english: I wanted to go out, but it started to rain.

sentences.spanish: Era tarde cuando llegamos.
sentences.english: It was late when we arrived.

sentences.spanish: Hacía frío y empezó a nevar.
sentences.english: It was cold and started to snow.

sentences.spanish: Yo de niño bebía mucha leche.
sentences.english: As a child I drank a lot of milk.

sentences.spanish: Anoche comí una pizza entera.
sentences.english: Last night I ate a whole pizza.

sentences.spanish: Mientras cantaban, entró el profesor.
sentences.english: While they were singing, the teacher entered.

sentences.spanish: Siempre íbamos al cine los domingos.
sentences.english: We always went to the movies on Sundays.

sentences.spanish: Fui a la tienda tres veces ayer.
sentences.english: I went to the store three times yesterday.

sentences.spanish: Nos conocimos en la fiesta el año pasado.
sentences.english: We met at the party last year.

sentences.spanish: Ustedes querían viajar pero no tenían dinero.
sentences.english: You all wanted to travel but didn't have money.

sentences.spanish: El teléfono sonó mientras me duchaba.
sentences.english: The phone rang while I was showering.

sentences.spanish: Leí el libro en dos horas.
sentences.english: I read the book in two hours.

sentences.spanish: Me sentí mal porque estaba enfermo.
sentences.english: I felt bad because I was sick.

sentences.spanish: Trabajábamos cuando oímos el ruido.
sentences.english: We were working when we heard the noise.

sentences.spanish: Tenía cinco años cuando tuve mi primer perro.
sentences.english: I was five years old when I got my first dog.

sentences.spanish: Ella era alta y tenía pelo castaño.
sentences.english: She was tall and had brown hair.

grammarExplanation: Use the Preterite for completed actions, specific times, or interrupting actions (e.g., salí, fuimos). Use the Imperfect for ongoing background actions, descriptions in the past, age/time, or repeated habitual actions (e.g., llovía, era, jugaba). Verbs like 'saber' and 'conocer' change meaning based on tense: 'sabía' (I knew), 'supe' (I found out), 'conocía' (I knew someone), 'conocí' (I met someone).

hacía.explanation: Imperfect of hacer (to make/do). Used for weather.

eran.explanation: Imperfect of ser (to be) for plural times or they were.

estaba.explanation: Imperfect of estar (to be) for states/locations.

conocí.explanation: Preterite of conocer. In preterite it means 'met (for the first time)'.

sabía.explanation: Imperfect of saber. In imperfect it means 'knew (ongoing knowledge)'.
